mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for equities, directly reducing present value of future cash flows and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden jump in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth, driving corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in greater than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with stable unemployment indicate successful inflation fighting without recession, supporting equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in greater than 65% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5% signals severe demand contraction, prompting immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in total nonfarm payrolls expand aggregate wage income and consumer spending capacity, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with 60-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices alongside falling retail sales indicate stagflationary pressure, eroding real purchasing power and corporate margins to produce negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL-RSAFS divergence yields positive 20-day returns in greater than 55% of observations.
